wallabag.name: wallabag
wallabag.short_description: With wallabag, archive the web freely. wallabag is a self hostable application for saving web pages.

features.description: Apps, browser extensions, RSS readers, and e-reader support.
features.title: Multi-platform apps and extensions

features.description: Can be self-hosted.
features.title: Self-hosting option

features.description: Dedicated guides for migrating an existing library.
features.title: Migration tools from Pocket and Omnivore

features.description: Saved articles are readable offline once synced to a client.
features.title: Offline reading

pros: Provides dedicated migration tools from both Pocket and Omnivore, not just a generic import
Supports e-readers and RSS readers, not just a mobile app
cons: Requires self-hosting (or a third-party host) to use, unlike a fully managed read-it-later service

faqs.answer: Yes — Wallabag provides a dedicated migration guide for moving from Pocket.
faqs.question: Can I migrate from Pocket to Wallabag?

notion.name: Notion
notion.short_description: An AI-powered workspace combining docs, wikis, project management, and databases into one connected tool teams use to run their company.

features.description: Any page can embed databases, tasks, images, or other pages as building blocks.
features.title: Block-based pages

features.description: The same underlying data can be viewed as a table, board, calendar, or timeline.
features.title: Flexible database views

features.description: Configurable AI agents that automate specific workflows within the workspace.
features.title: AI Custom Agents

features.description: Ask questions over a team's own Notion content and get AI-assisted writing help.
features.title: AI-assisted writing and Q&A

pros: Combines docs, wikis, tasks, and databases in one connected workspace instead of several point tools
Flexible database views (table, board, calendar, timeline) adapt to how a team actually works
cons: Steeper setup investment than a single-purpose tool — getting the most out of it requires structuring the workspace deliberately

faqs.answer: Yes — Notion has a free plan; paid Plus, Business, and Enterprise plans add collaboration limits, admin controls, and advanced features.
faqs.question: Is Notion free to use?

faqs.answer: Yes — including AI-assisted writing, Q&A over your own Notion content, and configurable Custom Agents.
faqs.question: Does Notion have AI features?

openarchiver.name: openarchiver
openarchiver.short_description: Open Archiver is the leading open-source email archiving solution. A secure, self-hosted platform for email backup, compliance, and eDiscovery with full-text search ability. Get this free email archiver today.

features.description: Direct connections to major email platforms.
features.title: Microsoft 365 and Google Workspace connectors

features.description: Import existing archives without a live mailbox connection.
features.title: PST and EML import

pros: Supports both live connections (Microsoft 365, Google Workspace, IMAP) and historical PST/EML imports

faqs.answer: Yes — along with generic IMAP servers and PST/EML file imports.
faqs.question: Does Open Archiver connect to Microsoft 365 and Google Workspace?

craft.name: Craft
craft.short_description: Craft is a notes and docs app with tasks, whiteboards, and AI writing tools, syncing across an individual's devices.

features.description: Rich document creation and editing.

features.description: Task management embedded directly inside documents.
features.title: Tasks

features.description: Built-in schedule viewing and planning.

features.description: Visual collaboration canvases.

features.description: Direct publishing of documents.
features.title: Publish & Share

features.description: Pre-built document templates.
features.title: Templates

pros: Free plan includes 15 AI credits and 1GB storage
Recognized as Apple's Mac App of the Year and an Apple Design Awards finalist
Family and Team plans bundle multiple Plus accounts for shared spaces
Integrates with Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, VS Code, and Apple Shortcuts
cons: Free plan is capped at 1,500 blocks of content and only 15 AI credits
No stated Android platform support on the site
AI credits are limited even on the paid Plus plan (50/month)

faqs.answer: €4.8/month billed monthly, or €8.0/month billed yearly.

faqs.answer: Yes, it connects with Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, VS Code, and Apple Shortcuts.

faqs.answer: Yes, the Family plan bundles 2-6 Plus accounts, and the Team plan bundles up to 10.

vanta.name: Vanta
vanta.short_description: Vanta is a compliance automation platform that continuously monitors security controls and streamlines audits for SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, and other frameworks.

features.description: Continuous monitoring and status tracking across multiple compliance frameworks.
features.title: Automated Compliance

features.description: Centralized visualization and management of organizational risk.
features.title: Risk Management

features.description: Vendor assessment and ongoing monitoring.
features.title: Third-Party Risk Management (TPRM)

features.description: Streamlined audit preparation and automated evidence collection.
features.title: Audit Automation

features.description: AI-powered responses to incoming security questionnaires.
features.title: Questionnaire Automation

features.description: Public-facing dashboard that showcases an organization's compliance status.
features.title: Trust Center

features.description: AI assistant for drafting policies, completing questionnaires, and flagging compliance issues.
features.title: Vanta AI Agent

pros: Supports SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, GDPR, HITRUST, FedRAMP, CMMC, and 15+ other frameworks
AI agent automates policy drafting and security questionnaire responses
Integrates with 400+ tools
Used by 16,000+ customers across startups, mid-market, and enterprise
cons: Pricing across all tiers (Essentials, Plus, Professional, Enterprise) is quote-based and not published
Comparing plans requires requesting a demo since feature-to-price mapping isn't public
Company size is not disclosed on the site

faqs.answer: No, all Vanta plans (Essentials, Plus, Professional, Enterprise) require contacting sales for a custom quote.

faqs.answer: Vanta was founded in 2018.
faqs.question: When was Vanta founded?

faqs.answer: Vanta lists San Francisco and New York, USA as its primary U.S. offices and operates as remote-first, with additional offices in Sydney, Dublin, and London.

linkding.name: Linkding
linkding.short_description: Self-hosted bookmark manager designed to be minimal, fast and easy to deploy in a single Docker container.

features.description: Linkding automatically retrieves the title, description, icon and preview image for each saved bookmark.
features.title: Automatic metadata fetching

features.description: Creates local HTML snapshots of saved pages or archives them via the Internet Archive for later reference.
features.title: Website archiving

features.description: Apply tagging, archiving or deletion actions across multiple bookmarks at once.
features.title: Bulk editing

features.description: Supports importing and exporting bookmarks in the standard Netscape HTML format.
features.title: Import and export

features.description: Supports multiple user accounts with bookmark sharing between users.
features.title: Multi-user support

features.description: Official browser extensions for Firefox and Chrome allow saving bookmarks directly from the browser.
features.title: Browser extensions

features.description: A REST API is available for building custom scripts and third-party integrations.
features.title: REST API

pros: Free and open source
Deploys as a single Docker container with SQLite, minimal operational overhead
Automatic metadata fetching and website archiving
Official Firefox and Chrome browser extensions
REST API for custom integrations
cons: Requires self-hosting; no official managed cloud version
No native mobile apps, relies on browser extensions and the web UI
Users are responsible for their own backups and updates

faqs.answer: Yes, Linkding is free, open-source software that you self-host.
faqs.question: Is Linkding free?

faqs.answer: Linkding is designed to run in a single Docker container using SQLite as its database.
faqs.question: How do I install Linkding?

faqs.answer: Yes, official extensions are available for Firefox and Chrome.
faqs.question: Does Linkding have browser extensions?

faqs.answer: Yes, Linkding supports importing and exporting bookmarks in the Netscape HTML bookmark format.
faqs.question: Can I import my existing bookmarks?

faqs.answer: Yes, a REST API is provided for building custom scripts and integrations.
faqs.question: Does Linkding have an API?

webrecorder.name: Webrecorder
webrecorder.short_description: Nonprofit suite of open-source web archiving tools, including Browsertrix, ArchiveWeb.page and ReplayWeb.page, for capturing and replaying web pages.

features.description: An automated, browser-based crawling platform for creating interactive web archives at scale, offered with free trials and tiered plans.
features.title: Browsertrix crawling platform

features.description: A free Chrome extension and desktop app for recording complex, interactive web page behavior while browsing.
features.title: ArchiveWeb.page

features.description: A free, serverless playback tool for viewing archived WARC/WACZ files directly in the browser.
features.title: ReplayWeb.page

features.description: Can archive pages that require a login, along with interactive and social media content.
features.title: Login-protected page capture

features.description: Uses deduplication technology to reduce storage requirements for large web archives.
features.title: Deduplication

features.description: Provides open-source libraries, command-line tools and file format specifications on GitHub.
features.title: Open-source developer tools

pros: ArchiveWeb.page and ReplayWeb.page are free to use
Handles complex, interactive and login-protected web content that simple crawlers miss
Run by a nonprofit focused on digital preservation, used by libraries and universities
Deduplication reduces storage costs for large-scale archives
Open-source tools and file format specs available on GitHub
cons: Browsertrix's full crawling platform is a paid, tiered service beyond the free trial
Large-scale web archiving still requires technical setup and storage planning

faqs.answer: Some Webrecorder tools, including ArchiveWeb.page and ReplayWeb.page, are free. Browsertrix, its automated crawling platform, offers a free trial with paid tiered plans.
faqs.question: Is Webrecorder free?

faqs.answer: Browsertrix is Webrecorder's automated, browser-based crawling platform for creating interactive web archives at scale.
faqs.question: What is Browsertrix?

faqs.answer: Yes, its tools are built to capture login-protected pages and interactive web content, including social media posts.
faqs.question: Can Webrecorder archive login-protected pages?

faqs.answer: Webrecorder is used by libraries, universities, governments and cultural organizations for digital preservation.
faqs.question: Who uses Webrecorder?

faqs.answer: Yes, Webrecorder's developer tools, libraries and file format specifications are open source and available on GitHub.
faqs.question: Is Webrecorder open source?

permify.name: Permify
permify.short_description: Permify is an open-source, Zanzibar-inspired authorization service for building fine-grained RBAC, ABAC, and ReBAC access control.

features.description: Abstracts permission logic out of individual applications into a single service, based on the relationship-modeling approach of Google's Zanzibar paper.
features.title: Centralized authorization engine

features.description: Supports role-based (RBAC), attribute-based (ABAC), and relationship-based (ReBAC) access control within one schema language.
features.title: Multiple access control models

features.description: Permify's documentation reports access-check response times as fast as 10ms for permission evaluation.
features.title: Fast access checks

features.description: Isolated authorization logic can be configured per tenant within a single Permify deployment.
features.title: Multi-tenant support

features.description: Runs as a self-hosted, AGPL-3.0 licensed service, or as Permify Cloud, a managed offering with SOC2 and GDPR compliance across AWS, GCP, and Azure.
features.title: Self-hosted or managed cloud

features.description: Authorization rules are defined in a dedicated schema language that can be version-controlled alongside application code.
features.title: Schema-as-code modeling

pros: Open source under AGPL-3.0 with a fully self-hostable Community edition
Modeled on Google's Zanzibar authorization approach used for large-scale systems
Supports RBAC, ABAC, and ReBAC in a single unified engine
Backed by FusionAuth following its November 2025 acquisition, which committed to keeping Permify open source
Managed Permify Cloud option available for teams that don't want to self-host
cons: Now part of FusionAuth's product family, with a unified pricing model planned for 2026 that may change today's standalone offering
Managed Permify Cloud pricing is not publicly listed and requires contacting sales
Requires learning Permify's own schema language to model authorization logic
Best suited to teams already comfortable running a dedicated authorization microservice

faqs.answer: Yes. Permify's core authorization engine is open source under the AGPL-3.0 license and can be self-hosted for free. A paid, managed Permify Cloud offering is also available.
faqs.question: Is Permify free to use?

faqs.answer: Permify was acquired by FusionAuth in November 2025. FusionAuth has stated Permify remains open source and continues to be developed as a standalone authorization engine.
faqs.question: Who maintains Permify?

faqs.answer: Permify supports role-based access control (RBAC), attribute-based access control (ABAC), and relationship-based access control (ReBAC) within a single schema.
faqs.question: What authorization models does Permify support?

faqs.answer: Yes. Permify Cloud is a managed hosting option with SOC2 and GDPR compliance, available across AWS, GCP, and Azure regions.
faqs.question: Can Permify run in the cloud?

features: Managed hosting on AWS, GCP, or Azure

libreoffice.name: LibreOffice
libreoffice.short_description: LibreOffice is a free, open-source office suite with word processing, spreadsheet, presentation, and database tools.

features.description: Word processing application for creating and editing documents.
features.title: Writer

features.description: Spreadsheet application for data analysis and calculations.
features.title: Calc

features.description: Presentation software for building slide decks.

features.description: Database management application.

features.description: Editor for creating mathematical formulas.

pros: Completely free with no cost to use
Open source under the Mozilla Public License v2.0
Includes six integrated applications covering most office needs
Maintained by a global community under The Document Foundation
cons: Website does not specify supported operating systems in detail
No official paid support tier listed on the homepage
Formatting compatibility with Microsoft Office files can vary by document complexity

faqs.answer: Yes, LibreOffice is free and open-source software, licensed under the Mozilla Public License v2.0.
faqs.question: Is LibreOffice free?

faqs.answer: LibreOffice is developed by The Document Foundation with contributions from a global community.

faqs.answer: LibreOffice includes Writer (word processing), Calc (spreadsheets), Impress (presentations), Draw (diagramming), Base (databases), and Math (formula editing).
faqs.question: What applications are included in LibreOffice?

ethercalc.name: EtherCalc
ethercalc.short_description: EtherCalc is an open-source, self-hostable web spreadsheet for real-time collaborative editing.

features.description: Multiple users can edit the same spreadsheet simultaneously by sharing a URL.
features.title: Real-time collaborative spreadsheets

features.description: Provides a REST API for programmatic access to spreadsheet data.
features.title: REST API

features.description: Can be self-hosted using Docker Compose without requiring Redis or other external services.
features.title: Self-hosting via Docker

features.description: Supports importing and exporting spreadsheets in XLSX format.
features.title: XLSX import/export

features.description: Can also be deployed to Cloudflare Workers as an alternative to self-hosting.
features.title: Deployable to Cloudflare Workers

pros: Free and open source, self-hostable with Docker
Simple sharing model: send a URL to collaborate instantly
REST API allows programmatic integration
XLSX import/export for compatibility with spreadsheet files
cons: Uses a mix of several open-source licenses across different components rather than one unified license
Requires setting up a reverse proxy with TLS for internet-facing production use
Less feature-rich than commercial spreadsheet applications like Excel or Google Sheets

faqs.answer: Yes, EtherCalc is free and open source.
faqs.question: Is EtherCalc free?

faqs.answer: Yes, EtherCalc can be self-hosted using Docker Compose, or deployed to Cloudflare Workers.
faqs.question: Can EtherCalc be self-hosted?

faqs.answer: Yes, EtherCalc supports XLSX import and export.
faqs.question: Does EtherCalc support Excel files?

faqs.answer: You share the spreadsheet's URL with others, and they can edit it together in real time.
faqs.question: How do I collaborate on an EtherCalc spreadsheet?

velociraptor.name: Velociraptor
velociraptor.short_description: Velociraptor is a free, open-source digital forensics and incident response tool for endpoint monitoring and threat hunting.

features.description: Investigators write custom VQL queries to build targeted hunts and forensic collections tailored to specific investigations.
features.title: Velociraptor Query Language (VQL)

features.description: Collects forensic evidence simultaneously across multiple endpoints for incident response.
features.title: Targeted evidence collection

features.description: Monitors endpoint events such as logs, file changes, and processes, with centralized, indefinite storage of that event data.
features.title: Continuous endpoint monitoring

features.description: Ships with a library of forensic artifacts that can be used directly or customized for proactive threat hunting.
features.title: Built-in artifact library

features.description: Designed to run hunts across large deployments, with reported use against tens of thousands of endpoints in real incident response operations.
features.title: Large-scale hunts

pros: Fully open source under the AGPL license and free to use
Powerful custom query language (VQL) for building tailored forensic collections and hunts
Maintained by Rapid7, which acquired the Velociraptor team in 2021, providing ongoing development resources
Scales to large, distributed endpoint fleets for enterprise-wide incident response
cons: Rapid7 also integrates Velociraptor into its own commercial InsightIDR/XDR products, which are separate paid offerings from the free open-source tool
VQL has a learning curve for analysts unfamiliar with its query syntax
Requires self-hosting and operational management; there is no vendor-run SaaS version of the open-source tool itself

faqs.answer: Yes. Velociraptor is open source under the AGPL license and free to use.
faqs.question: Is Velociraptor free?

faqs.answer: Velociraptor is maintained by Rapid7, which acquired the Velociraptor team in 2021, though the tool remains free and open source.
faqs.question: Who maintains Velociraptor?

faqs.answer: VQL (Velociraptor Query Language) is the query language used to write custom forensic collections and threat-hunting queries in Velociraptor.
faqs.question: What is VQL?

faqs.answer: Velociraptor is used for endpoint monitoring and forensics across large, distributed fleets of endpoints in incident response operations.
faqs.question: What platforms does Velociraptor support?

fleetdm.name: FleetDM
fleetdm.short_description: Fleet (FleetDM) is an open-source device management platform built on osquery for managing macOS, Linux, Windows, iOS, and Android.

features.description: Manages device inventory across macOS, Linux, Windows, iOS, and Android from a single console.
features.title: Cross-platform device inventory

features.description: Fleet's site states devices can report data back in under 30 seconds.
features.title: Real-time device reporting

features.description: Provides software update and patch management alongside vulnerability visibility across managed devices.
features.title: Patch and vulnerability management

features.description: Device configuration and policies can be managed as code, and automated via a single REST API.
features.title: Infrastructure as code

features.description: Can be run as a self-hosted open-source deployment or as a hosted cloud service.
features.title: Cloud or self-hosted deployment

features.description: Supports live reports, scripting, and diagnostics against endpoints, built on the osquery agent.
features.title: Live queries and diagnostics

pros: Free, open-source version is available and stated to always remain free
Manages a wide range of operating systems (macOS, Linux, Windows, iOS, Android) from one console
Fleet states it is used by 1,300+ organizations across 90+ countries managing 2,000,000+ devices
Offers both self-hosted and cloud deployment options
Single API supports automation and infrastructure-as-code workflows
cons: Premium tier pricing is not publicly detailed on the marketing site and requires contacting sales
Advanced features (beyond the free/open-source tier) require a paid premium license
Self-hosting requires infrastructure and operational overhead compared to a fully managed MDM

faqs.answer: Fleet maintains a free, open-source version that the company states will always be free, alongside paid premium tiers with additional features.
faqs.question: Is Fleet free?

faqs.answer: Fleet can manage macOS, Linux, Windows, iOS, and Android devices from a single console.
faqs.question: What operating systems does Fleet manage?

faqs.answer: Yes. Fleet can be deployed self-hosted or used as a hosted cloud service.
faqs.question: Can Fleet be self-hosted?

faqs.answer: Fleet's site states it is used by 1,300+ organizations across 90+ countries, managing over 2,000,000 devices.
